angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Fishing conditions on the Toccoa River are fair to good with recent stocking enhancing trout activity. Expect trout to feed comfortably near runs, seams, and structure with balanced nymph tactics effective.
generated_text.best_window: The best window is day1 morning due to low rain probability, moderate sky cover, and calm conditions supporting steady trout feeding and effective use of balanced nymph approaches near runs and seams.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current USGS conditions show flow at 227 cfs, gage height 1.2 ft, water temperature unavailable, and turbidity data unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages around 30%, pressure is steady at 30.08 inHg with no strong trend, wind averages 3.5 mph with max gusts near 14 mph, and light rain chance at 0.1 inches is possible later evening.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will likely hold near runs, seams, and structures, favoring clean drifts with smaller natural patterns. Use stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell nymphs to match recent stocking; exercise caution with variable wind gusts in the evening.
